title: Adults Who Cannot Read or Write
transcript: Part 1 You're going to hear five people. You'll hear each person only once. First read items 41 to 45. After you've listened to each person, decide if the statement for each of the items 41 to 45 is true or not true. Mark your answer on the answer sheet. Now you will have half a minute to read the five statements. You're going to hear five people talking about the problem of adults who cannot read or write. Number 41. I can't really say that it's a serious problem because I haven't been directly affected by it, but I suppose there must be people who can't read or write. It could be a problem down in the south, but I don't understand how big city people could get by without being able to read. Number 42. Absolutely. In my line of work, there have been times when I've had to read out statements to people. I've seen people sign X for their names, and among those who can sign their names, most can't even read basic English. It's a worldwide problem. People should recognise the seriousness of the problem. Number 43. It's a great pity that so many people can't read or write. The problem lies in a breakdown in our school system. We spend so much on other things. We should spend more on education. Number 44. There are probably millions of high school children who have reading and writing problems. For years, students have been allowed to continue their studies even though they weren't really prepared for a higher level. As a result, there are some students who can't read very well or at all.

statements.statement: The first speaker has thought a lot about the problem.
statements.audio_transcript: I can't really say that it's a serious problem because I haven't been directly affected by it, but I suppose there must be people who can't read or write. It could be a problem down in the south, but I don't understand how big city people could get by without being able to read.

explanation.de: FALSCH. Der Sprecher sagt, er könne nicht sagen, dass es ein ernstes Problem sei, da er nicht direkt davon betroffen war. Seine vagen Formulierungen wie «I suppose» und «I don't understand» zeigen, dass er sich kaum mit dem Thema beschäftigt hat.
explanation.en: INCORRECT. The speaker says 'I can't really say that it's a serious problem because I haven't been directly affected by it.' His vague language like 'I suppose' and 'I don't understand' shows he hasn't given it much thought.
explanation.ar: خطأ. يقول المتحدث إنه 'لا يستطيع القول إنها مشكلة خطيرة لأنه لم يتأثر بها مباشرة'. عباراته الغامضة مثل 'أفترض' و'لا أفهم' تظهر أنه لم يفكر كثيراً في الموضوع.
explanation.fr: FAUX. Le locuteur dit qu'il 'ne peut pas vraiment dire que c'est un problème grave parce qu'il n'a pas été directement concerné'. Ses formulations vagues comme 'I suppose' et 'I don't understand' montrent qu'il n'y a pas beaucoup réfléchi.
explanation.es: INCORRECTO. El hablante dice que 'no puede decir realmente que sea un problema serio porque no le ha afectado directamente'. Sus expresiones vagas como 'supongo' y 'no entiendo' muestran que no ha pensado mucho en el tema.
explanation.ru: НЕПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит, что 'не может сказать, что это серьёзная проблема, потому что он не был затронут напрямую'. Его расплывчатые формулировки «I suppose» и «I don't understand» показывают, что он мало думал об этом.

statements.statement: The second speaker has personal experience with people unable to read or write.
statements.audio_transcript: Absolutely. In my line of work, there have been times when I've had to read out statements to people. I've seen people sign X for their names, and among those who can sign their names, most can't even read basic English. It's a worldwide problem. People should recognise the seriousness of the problem.

explanation.de: RICHTIG. Der Sprecher sagt «in my line of work» habe er Leuten Erklärungen vorlesen müssen und gesehen, wie sie mit einem X unterschrieben. Das ist direkte persönliche Erfahrung mit Menschen, die nicht lesen oder schreiben können.
explanation.en: CORRECT. The speaker says 'in my line of work, there have been times when I've had to read out statements to people' and 'I've seen people sign X for their names'. This is direct personal experience with people who cannot read or write.
explanation.ar: صحيح. يقول المتحدث 'في عملي، كانت هناك أوقات اضطررت فيها لقراءة بيانات للناس' و'رأيت أشخاصاً يوقعون بعلامة X بدلاً من أسمائهم'. هذه تجربة شخصية مباشرة.
explanation.fr: CORRECT. Le locuteur dit 'dans mon travail, j'ai dû lire des déclarations à des gens' et 'j'ai vu des gens signer avec un X'. C'est une expérience personnelle directe avec des personnes illettrées.
explanation.es: CORRECTO. El hablante dice 'en mi trabajo, ha habido veces en que tuve que leer declaraciones a la gente' y 'he visto personas firmar con una X en lugar de su nombre'. Esta es experiencia personal directa con personas analfabetas.
explanation.ru: ПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит: 'в моей работе бывали случаи, когда мне приходилось зачитывать людям заявления' и 'я видел, как люди ставят X вместо подписи'. Это прямой личный опыт.

statements.statement: The third speaker is not satisfied with the present-day school system.
statements.audio_transcript: It's a great pity that so many people can't read or write. The problem lies in a breakdown in our school system. We spend so much on other things. We should spend more on education.

explanation.de: RICHTIG. Der Sprecher sagt, das Problem liege in «einem Zusammenbruch unseres Schulsystems» und wir sollten «mehr für Bildung ausgeben». Das zeigt deutlich seine Unzufriedenheit mit dem gegenwärtigen Schulsystem.
explanation.en: CORRECT. The speaker says 'the problem lies in a breakdown in our school system' and 'we should spend more on education'. This clearly shows dissatisfaction with the current school system.
explanation.ar: صحيح. يقول المتحدث إن 'المشكلة تكمن في انهيار نظامنا المدرسي' و'يجب أن ننفق المزيد على التعليم'. هذا يظهر بوضوح عدم رضاه عن النظام المدرسي الحالي.
explanation.fr: CORRECT. Le locuteur dit que 'le problème réside dans une défaillance de notre système scolaire' et qu''on devrait dépenser plus pour l'éducation'. Cela montre clairement son insatisfaction envers le système scolaire actuel.
explanation.es: CORRECTO. El hablante dice que 'el problema radica en un fallo de nuestro sistema escolar' y 'deberíamos gastar más en educación'. Esto muestra claramente su insatisfacción con el sistema escolar actual.
explanation.ru: ПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит, что 'проблема в разрушении нашей школьной системы' и 'мы должны тратить больше на образование'. Это явно показывает недовольство современной школьной системой.

statements.statement: The fourth speaker feels that enough is already being done to help people who cannot read or write.
statements.audio_transcript: There are probably millions of high school children who have reading and writing problems. For years, students have been allowed to continue their studies even though they weren't really prepared for a higher level.

explanation.de: FALSCH. Der Sprecher beschreibt ein Systemversagen: Schüler durften «jahrelang ihre Studien fortsetzen, obwohl sie nicht wirklich auf ein höheres Niveau vorbereitet waren». Das zeigt, dass nicht genug getan wird – das Gegenteil der Aussage.
explanation.en: INCORRECT. The speaker describes a systemic failure: 'For years, students have been allowed to continue their studies even though they weren't really prepared for a higher level.' This shows that NOT enough is being done — the opposite of what the statement claims.
explanation.ar: خطأ. يصف المتحدث فشلاً منهجياً: 'لسنوات، سُمح للطلاب بمواصلة دراستهم رغم أنهم لم يكونوا مستعدين لمستوى أعلى'. هذا يظهر أنه لا يُفعل ما يكفي.
explanation.fr: FAUX. Le locuteur décrit une défaillance systémique : 'Pendant des années, les élèves ont été autorisés à poursuivre leurs études même s'ils n'étaient pas vraiment préparés pour un niveau supérieur.' Cela montre que ce n'est PAS suffisant.
explanation.es: INCORRECTO. El hablante describe un fallo sistémico: 'Durante años, se ha permitido a los estudiantes continuar sus estudios aunque no estaban realmente preparados para un nivel superior.' Esto muestra que NO se está haciendo lo suficiente.
explanation.ru: НЕПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий описывает системный провал: 'Годами ученикам позволяли продолжать учёбу, хотя они не были готовы к более высокому уровню.' Это показывает, что делается НЕДОСТАТОЧНО.

statements.statement: The fifth speaker thinks all students can read and write properly.
statements.audio_transcript: As a result, there are some students who can't read very well or at all.

explanation.de: FALSCH. Der Sprecher sagt ausdrücklich, es gebe Schüler, die «nicht sehr gut oder überhaupt nicht lesen können». Das ist das direkte Gegenteil der Behauptung, alle Schüler könnten richtig lesen und schreiben.
explanation.en: INCORRECT. The speaker explicitly states that 'there are some students who can't read very well or at all'. This directly contradicts the claim that all students can read and write properly.
explanation.ar: خطأ. يقول المتحدث صراحةً إن 'هناك بعض الطلاب الذين لا يستطيعون القراءة جيداً أو على الإطلاق'. هذا يناقض مباشرة الادعاء بأن جميع الطلاب يستطيعون القراءة والكتابة.
explanation.fr: FAUX. Le locuteur dit explicitement qu''il y a des étudiants qui ne savent pas très bien lire, voire pas du tout'. Cela contredit directement l'affirmation selon laquelle tous les étudiants savent lire et écrire correctement.
explanation.es: INCORRECTO. El hablante dice explícitamente que 'hay algunos estudiantes que no pueden leer muy bien o no pueden leer en absoluto'. Esto contradice directamente la afirmación de que todos los estudiantes saben leer y escribir correctamente.
explanation.ru: НЕПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий прямо говорит, что 'есть ученики, которые не могут читать хорошо или вообще'. Это прямо противоречит утверждению, что все ученики умеют читать и писать.
